metadata.description: As the inherited plan for a tree‚Äö√Ñ√¥s structure, an architectural model markedly affects the shape of a tree. This study assigned architectural models to 13 species found in two common forest types on Moorea, Inocarpus-dominated mid-elevation forest and Hibiscus- and Cocos- dominated coastal forest. Trunks and crowns of every tree taller than 3 meters were plotted on top-down drawings and profiles of five 50 m2 plots from each forest type. The total area covered by each species and architectural model was determined for each plot, and the two forest types were found to be dominated by trees with different architectural models. Light penetration was measured in each forest type, but no significant difference was found between penetration of the two forest types.
metadata.title: A survey of tree and forest architecture in Moorea
